contradiction_text: LoCoMo is presented as a strong test of very long-term conversational memory, but the conversations are not raw natural dialogue; they are generated through an LLM pipeline and then materially corrected by humans.
related_claim_texts: LoCoMo is not a purely naturalistic corpus, because its conversations are generated through an LLM-based pipeline and then materially revised by human annotators to repair inconsistencies.
LoCoMo is a deliberately constructed benchmark for testing long-horizon conversational memory across months of interaction.
disclosure: Keep visible that the benchmark’s value as a controlled long-memory stress test does not make it straightforward evidence about raw real-world longitudinal conversation, because nearly 15% of turns and about 19% of images were corrected after generation.

contradiction_text: More context and more retrieval do not reliably improve performance; selective observation retrieval helps, but expanding retrieved material or using longer-context settings can coincide with worse results.
related_claim_texts: Retrieval helps on LoCoMo when it is selective and observation-focused, but more retrieved context or longer history does not reliably improve performance and can coincide with degradation.
Reported models remain far below human performance on LoCoMo QA, indicating that very long-term conversational memory and reasoning are still a substantial unsolved problem on this benchmark.
disclosure: Keep visible that LoCoMo does not support a simple “more memory surface helps” story: top-5 observation retrieval beats broader retrieval, top-50 retrieval degrades QA, event summarization is better in one smaller-context setting than a larger-context one, and multimodal relevance drops as dialogue history grows.

contradiction_text: Long-context exposure is supposed to support long-horizon memory, yet one reported long-context adversarial QA setting collapses to extremely poor performance while a shorter-context base setting performs far better.
related_claim_texts: Reported models remain far below human performance on LoCoMo QA, indicating that very long-term conversational memory and reasoning are still a substantial unsolved problem on this benchmark.
Retrieval helps on LoCoMo when it is selective and observation-focused, but more retrieved context or longer history does not reliably improve performance and can coincide with degradation.
disclosure: Keep visible that longer context is not functioning here as a reliable proxy for stronger memory reasoning: the human-model gap remains large overall, and adversarial QA can collapse to 2.1 F1 in a reported 16K setting while GPT-4-turbo in a 4K base setting reaches 70.2.
